concept: Usable / Able to Perform a Stated Purpose in Practice
description: Able to be operated or used for a stated purpose by a stated person under the relevant conditions. Something can be available yet confusing, painful, unreliable, or otherwise unusable in practice. 'lokane' (accessible) includes reaching the opportunity for use; 'kelasu' begins at the point of use itself.
sound_symbolism: Crisp 'ke' makes contact with the object, then liquid 'la' tests how action moves through it. With 'su', that test settles into practical use.
grammatical_notes: A predicative or attributive adjective: 'ha keli kelasu nai.' (This device is usable.) The clause can name the user, purpose, conditions, and evidence. The quality-noun rule supplies usability as a property under those limits.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Repairable community tools matter only when people can operate them for the work they actually need to do.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: The adjective gives a person a plain way to challenge the claim that provision alone solved the problem.

tags.activity: practical use for a stated person and purpose
tags.cognition: use that remains understandable enough to carry through
tags.creation: a designed object or process that can be operated in practice
